questions.question: What is the GCF of $12$ and $18$?
In ancient Egypt, the Pyramids of Giza were built with stones weighing $12$, $18$, or $24$ tons. What is the GCF of these weights?
The prime factorization of $42$ is $2 \cdot 3 \cdot 7$. What is the LCM of $42$ and $63$?
A Mayan calendar has $15$ days in a week and $20$ weeks in a month. What is the LCM of $15$ and $20$?
A Greek architect is designing a temple with columns spaced $8$ meters apart and a hallway $12$ meters wide. What is the GCF of $8$ and $12$?
The prime factorization of $48$ is $2^4 \cdot 3$. What is the LCM of $48$ and $75$?

In the construction of the Great Wall of China, stones were placed $10$ meters apart. If a watchtower was built every $25$ meters, what is the LCM of $10$ and $25$? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

An ancient Babylonian farmer had $18$ baskets of dates and $24$ baskets of figs. If he wanted to pack the baskets in rows with the same number of baskets in each row, what is the GCF of $18$ and $24$? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

teacher_rules: Provide clear instructions for each problem
Allow students to use calculators for checking
Encourage students to show their work and explain their reasoning
